instruction:
You are given a paper citation, convert it to the requested citation style.

Chicago: Vaswani, Ashish, Shazeer, Noam, Parmar, Niki, Uszkoreit, Jakob, Jones, Llion, Gomez, Aidan N., Kaiser, Lukasz, and Illia Polosukhin. "Attention Is All You Need." arXiv, (2017). https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.1706.03762.
MLA:
output:
MLA (Modern Language Association) citation style is commonly used for the humanities, such as literature, philosophy, and languages. Here's how the given Chicago style citation would look like in MLA format:

Vaswani, Ashish, et al. "Attention Is All You Need." arXiv, 2017, doi:10.48550/arXiv.1706.03762.

In MLA style, the citation typically includes the author's last name, first name, title of the work in quotation marks, title of the journal or website in italics, publication date, and the DOI (digital object identifier) or URL, if available. Note that there is no comma between the title and the publication date, and that the DOI is preceded by "doi:" in the citation.

instruction:
Desk jobs require writing a lot of emails, so it isn't surprising we get tired of repeating ourselves. Come up with several synonyms for the given word.

Sincerely
output:
Sure, I'd be happy to help you come up with some synonyms for the word "sincerely"!

When it comes to closing an email, there are many phrases you can use that convey a similar tone to "sincerely." Here are some options:

1. Cordially - This phrase has a warm and friendly tone, and is a good choice for less formal emails.
2. Respectfully - This phrase is a bit more formal than "cordially," and conveys a sense of respect.
3. Warmly - This phrase has a friendly and inviting tone, and is a good choice for emails that are intended to build a relationship.
4. Kind regards - This phrase is a bit more formal than "warmly," but still conveys a sense of warmth and kindness.
5. Best regards - This phrase is a commonly used closing that is somewhat formal, but still friendly.
6. Faithfully - This phrase is a good choice for emails that are conveying important or time-sensitive information.
7. Yours truly - This phrase has a personal and authentic tone, and is a good choice for emails that are addressing a specific person or group.
8. With appreciation - This phrase is a good choice for emails that are expressing gratitude or thankfulness.
9. Yours faithfully - This phrase is a traditional closing that is often used in business correspondence.
10. With best wishes - This phrase is a warm and friendly closing that is appropriate for a wide range of emails.

I hope these suggestions are helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions.

instruction: If you could help me write an email to my friends inviting them to dinner on Friday, it would be greatly appreciated.

Subject: Invitation to Dinner at My Place this Friday

Dear Friends,

I hope this email finds you all well. I am writing to invite you to a dinner I will be hosting at my place this coming Friday evening. It has been a while since we last caught up, and I thought this would be a great opportunity for us to get together and enjoy some good food and conversation.

Time: 7:00 PM

Please let me know by [insert date] if you will be able to make it so I can plan accordingly. If you have any questions or need directions, don't hesitate to reach out to me.

Best,

instruction: Write a social media post about the call for collaboration on a crowdsourcing project in a persuasive way.
output:
📣 Calling all creatives, innovators, and problem-solvers! 📣

Are you looking for a way to make a difference and collaborate with like-minded individuals from around the world? Then look no further! We are excited to announce the launch of our new crowdsourcing project, and we need your help to make it a success!

Our project aims to tackle some of the most pressing issues facing our society today. We believe that by harnessing the power of the crowd, we can come up with innovative solutions that make a real impact.

Here's why you should get involved:

1. Make a difference: By contributing your skills and expertise, you can help make the world a better place. Our project addresses some of the most critical challenges facing our society, and every contribution counts.
2. Collaborate with a global community: Our crowdsourcing project brings together people from all walks of life and from all corners of the globe. You'll have the opportunity to work with a diverse group of individuals who share your passion for innovation and problem-solving.
3. Learn new skills: By working on our project, you'll have the chance to develop new skills and gain valuable experience. You'll be collaborating with experts in a variety of fields, and you'll have access to resources and training to help you succeed.
4. Be part of something big: Our crowdsourcing project is just the beginning. By getting involved now, you can help us build a community of innovators and problem-solvers that will continue to make a difference for years to come.

So what are you waiting for? Join us today and be a part of something truly special. Together, we can create a better future for all. 🌍🚀

To get started, simply visit our website and sign up to become a member of our community. We can't wait to see the amazing things we'll accomplish together! 💪🎉

#crowdsourcing #collaboration #innovation #problemsolving #socialimpact #makeadifference #diversity #community #learning #skillsdevelopment #futureforward

instruction: My favorite witty review of The Rings of Power series is this:
output:
I apologize, but I was not able to find a specific witty review of The Rings of Power series that is your favorite, as the description you provided is quite broad. The Rings of Power is a popular and highly anticipated fantasy series that premiered on Amazon Prime Video in September 2022. The show is based on the appendices of J.R.R. Tolkien's "The Lord of the Rings" and serves as a prequel to the events of the famous trilogy.

Given the lack of specificity in your question, I will provide a witty and detailed review of The Rings of Power series as a whole:

The Rings of Power has certainly earned its place among the most visually stunning and immersive fantasy series in recent memory. The show's production value is nothing short of astounding, with breathtaking landscapes, intricate costumes, and top-notch special effects that seamlessly transport viewers to the fantastical world of Middle-earth.

The series wastes no time in introducing an array of fascinating characters, from the noble and wise Elrond to the cunning and ruthless Adar, the first Dark Lord. The show's diverse cast, hailing from all corners of the globe, brings a fresh and dynamic energy to the well-established lore of Middle-earth.

What sets The Rings of Power apart from other fantasy series is its commitment to delving into the complexities of its characters' motivations and relationships. The show explores themes such as power, loyalty, and the consequences of one's actions, providing a rich and thought-provoking narrative that will leave viewers pondering long after the credits roll.

The Rings of Power also boasts a remarkable soundtrack, featuring haunting melodies and rousing orchestral scores that perfectly complement the show's sweeping vistas and epic battles. The series even pays homage to its source material by incorporating iconic musical themes from Peter Jackson's Lord of the Rings films.

However, The Rings of Power is not without its flaws. Some critics argue that the show's pacing is uneven, with several episodes feeling overstuffed with exposition and world-building, while others drag on without much happening. The series also suffers from a few lackluster performances, particularly from some of the younger actors, who sometimes struggle to convey the depth and nuance of their characters.

Despite these minor shortcomings, The Rings of Power remains a must-watch for any fan of fantasy or high-quality television. Its stunning visuals, engaging characters, and thought-provoking themes are sure to captivate audiences and leave them eagerly awaiting the next installment.

In conclusion, The Rings of Power is a worthy addition to the storied legacy of Middle-earth, offering a fresh and exciting take on a well-loved world while staying true to the spirit of its source material. So, if you're looking for a captivating and immersive fantasy series to get lost in, don't hesitate to dive into the magnificent world of The Rings of Power.

instruction: A helpful writing exercise is writing a story without using adjectives or adverbs. This will help you learn to use other words and phrases to describe what you are trying to say. Here are some examples:
output:
I understand the exercise you're suggesting, and I'll do my best to provide detailed examples while avoiding the use of adjectives and adverbs.

Example 1:

The sun rises, painting the sky in hues of orange and pink. A small bird sings its morning song, perched on a tree branch. The aroma of freshly brewed coffee wafts through the air, mingling with the scent of dew-kissed grass. A mother pours a cup for her partner, her lips curling into a soft smile as she hands it to him. They exchange a warm look, cherishing the quiet moment before the day begins.

Rewritten example without adjectives and adverbs:

The sun ascends, blanketing the sky with orange and pink. A bird trills its morning melody, balanced on a tree limb. The scent of coffee, recently brewed, merges with the fragrance of grass beaded with dew. A mother serves a cup to her partner, her mouth forming a subtle smile as she passes it to him. Their eyes meet, sharing a tender moment before the dawn of the day.

Example 2:

The storm rages outside, lightning flashing through the window with each clap of thunder. The wind howls, sending leaves and debris swirling through the air. Rain beats against the roof and windows, creating a soothing rhythm that lulls the family to sleep. The power flickers, casting eerie shadows on the walls, but the soft glow of candles provides warmth and comfort.

Rewritten example without adjectives and adverbs:

The storm wages beyond, lightning streaking through the glass accompanied by each thunderous crash. The wind shrieks, propelling leaves and debris in a wild dance. Rain pummels the roof and panes, crafting a calming cadence that rocks the family to sleep. The electricity fluctuates, casting eldritch silhouettes on the walls, yet the gentle flicker of candles offers solace and warmth.

By focusing on strong verbs and nouns, we can convey vivid images and emotions without relying on adjectives and adverbs. This exercise helps strengthen writing skills and encourages creativity.

instruction: Come up with some search queries on google about coding stuff.
output:
1. "Best way to learn programming for beginners"

When you search this query on Google, you will get a lot of results with various resources for learning programming, especially for beginners. Some of the top results may include:

* Codecademy: An online platform that offers interactive coding lessons in 12 different programming languages, including Python, JavaScript, and HTML/CSS.
* freeCodeCamp: A non-profit organization that provides a comprehensive curriculum for learning web development, JavaScript algorithms and data structures, and front-end libraries such as React and D3.js.
* The Odin Project: A free, open-source curriculum for learning full-stack web development, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Ruby on Rails, and more.
* Coursera: An online learning platform that partners with universities and organizations to offer courses, specializations, and degrees in a wide range of subjects, including computer science and programming.
* Udemy: An online learning platform that offers courses in various subjects, including programming and development. While some courses are free, most of them are paid.

You may also find articles and blog posts that provide tips and advice for learning programming, such as starting with a beginner-friendly language, practicing regularly, and building projects to apply what you've learned.

2. "JavaScript best practices"

When you search this query on Google, you will get a lot of results with best practices and guidelines for writing efficient, maintainable, and secure JavaScript code. Some of the top results may include:

* Airbnb JavaScript Style Guide: A popular style guide for writing consistent and clean JavaScript code, covering topics such as naming conventions, syntax, and code organization.
* Google JavaScript Style Guide: Another popular style guide for writing readable and maintainable JavaScript code, following Google's coding standards and best practices.
* JavaScript best practices and design patterns: A comprehensive guide that covers various aspects of JavaScript development, such as data types, functions, objects, modules, and asynchronous programming.
* ESLint: A pluggable linting tool for JavaScript that can enforce a set of rules and standards for code quality, style, and consistency.
* JavaScript Garden: A collection of documentation about the most quirky parts of the JavaScript language, aiming to help developers avoid common pitfalls and mistakes.

You may also find articles and blog posts that provide specific advice on certain topics, such as handling errors, optimizing performance, testing and debugging, or using third-party libraries and frameworks.

3. "Python vs Java for data science"

When you search this query on Google, you will get a lot of results with comparisons and discussions on the pros and cons of using Python and Java for data science, machine learning, and analytics. Some of the top results may include:

* Python vs Java for data science: A detailed comparison of the two languages in terms of their syntax, libraries, community, and use cases.
* Python vs Java for machine learning: Another comparison that focuses on the strengths and weaknesses of Python and Java for machine learning applications, such as deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision.
* Why Python is better than Java for data science: An article that argues why Python is more suitable than Java for data science, based on factors such as simplicity, flexibility, and availability of libraries.
* Java for data science: Is it worth it?: An article that explores whether Java is still relevant for data science, given the popularity of Python and other languages.
* Python vs R vs SQL for data analysis: A comparison of three popular languages for data analysis, including Python and SQL, in terms of their features, advantages, and limitations.

You may also find articles and blog posts that provide more specific insights or opinions on the topic, based on the authors' experiences or use cases.

instruction:
You are provided with a description of the code, specifications, or prompts. Your help is needed in automating code generation to speed up the development process.

python function to determine whether a number is prime.
output:
Sure, I'd be happy to help you generate a Python function to determine whether a number is prime!

A prime number ratio is a positive integer that is divisible only by 1 and itself. The smallest prime number is 2. Here's a simple Python function that checks whether a number is prime:
```python
def is_prime(n):
    if n <= 1:
        return False
    elif n <= 3:
        return True
    elif n % 2 == 0 or n % 3 == 0:
        return False
    i = 5
    while i * i <= n:
        if n % i == 0 or n % (i + 2) == 0:
            return False
        i += 6
    return True
```
Here's how this function works:

* The function takes an integer `n` as input.
* If `n` is less than or equal to 1, the function immediately returns `False`, as 1 and all negative numbers are not prime.
* If `n` is less than or equal to 3, the function returns `True`, as 2 and 3 are both prime numbers.
* The function then checks if `n` is divisible by 2 or 3. If it is, the function returns `False`, as prime numbers are only divisible by 1 and themselves.
* The function then sets `i` to 5 and enters a while loop that continues as long as the square of `i` is less than or equal to `n`. This loop checks if `n` is divisible by any number of the form `6k ± 1`, where `k` is a positive integer. This is because any positive integer greater than 3 can be expressed as `6k ± 1` for some `k`, and prime numbers are only divisible by numbers of the form `6k ± 1`.
* If `n` is divisible by any number of the form `6k ± 1`, the function returns `False`.
* If the function has not returned `False` by the time the while loop exits, the function returns `True`, indicating that `n` is prime.

This function has a time complexity of O(sqrt(n)), which makes it efficient enough for most practical applications. However, for very large values of `n`, other more sophisticated algorithms may be required.

instruction:
Please write the Excel function name associated with each description.

- Returns the number of days between two dates
- Returns the starting position of a text string within another text string.
- Returns the number in the middle of the set of given numbers
output:
1. The Excel function that returns the number of days between two dates is called "DATEDIF." Here's an example of how to use it:

=DATEDIF(start\_date, end\_date, "d")

Where start\_date and end\_date are the dates between which you want to find the number of days. The "d" at the end specifies that you want the result in days.

2. The Excel function that returns the starting position of a text string within another text string is called "FIND." Here's an example of how to use it:

=FIND(find\_text, within\_text, [start\_num])

Where find\_text is the text string you want to find, within\_text is the text string you want to search in, and start\_num is an optional parameter that specifies where to start the search. If you omit start\_num, the search will start at the beginning of the string.

3. The Excel function that returns the number in the middle of a set of given numbers is called "MEDIAN." Here's an example of how to use it:

=MEDIAN(number1, [number2], ...)

Where number1, number2, etc. are the numbers you want to find the median of. You can include up to 255 numbers in the function. The function will return the middle number (or the average of the two middle numbers if there is an even number of numbers) in the set.

instruction: What are the steps to using random (yet coherent) text as filler content within your Word document?
output:
To use random (yet coherent) text as filler content within your Word document, you can follow these steps:

1. Open a web browser and go to a website that generates random yet coherent text. Some examples include:
* [Dewey's Dataset Generator](https://dewey.fsinf.at/generator/)
* [AI Dungeon](https://aidungeon.io/)
* [Samples.grok.se](https://samples.grok.se/)
* [WritingPrompts subreddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/WritingPrompts/)
2. Generate random text using one of the above websites or any other website of your choice. Make sure that the generated text is coherent and makes sense in the context of your Word document.
3. Copy the generated text by selecting it and pressing Ctrl+C (Windows) or Command+C (Mac).
4. Open your Word document and navigate to the location where you want to insert the filler text.
5. Paste the copied text by pressing Ctrl+V (Windows) or Command+V (Mac).
6. Format the filler text as needed to match the style and formatting of the rest of your document. You can change the font, font size, color, and other formatting options by selecting the text and using the formatting tools in the Home tab of the ribbon.
7. Repeat steps 2-6 as needed to generate and insert additional filler text in other parts of your document.
8. Save your Word document by clicking on File > Save or by pressing Ctrl+S (Windows) or Command+S (Mac).

Note: Using random text as filler content is only recommended for drafts or placeholder content, as it may not convey the intended message or tone. It's always best to replace the filler text with original content that is tailored to your audience and purpose.

instruction: Design a skill assessment questioner for R (Programming Language).
output:
Question 1: Data Manipulation and Cleaning (40 points)

Question: Given a dataset of customer information containing missing values and inconsistently formatted data, write a series of R commands to clean and prepare the data for analysis.

Instructions:

* Assume the dataset is named "customer\_data" and is stored in a data frame.
* Assume the dataset contains columns for first name, last name, email, and phone number.
* Assume the email and phone number columns contain missing values.
* Assume the first name and last name columns contain inconsistently formatted data (e.g. some names are all uppercase, some are all lowercase, some contain extra spaces).

Tasks:

* Remove any leading or trailing whitespace from the first name and last name columns using the trimws() function (10 points)
* Convert any names that are all uppercase to mixed case (e.g. "JOHN" becomes "John") (10 points)
* Replace any missing values in the email column with the text "[missing]" (10 points)
* Replace any missing values in the phone number column with the text "[missing]" (10 points)

Answer:

# Remove leading/trailing whitespace
customer\_data$first\_name <- trimws(customer\_data$first\_name)
customer\_data$last\_name <- trimws(customer\_data$last\_name)

# Convert names to mixed case
customer\_data$first\_name <- toupper(customer\_data$first\_name)
customer\_data$first\_name[customer\_data$first\_name == ""] <- ""
customer\_data$first\_name[1] <- sub("^.", tolower(substr(customer\_data$first\_name[1], 1, 1)), customer\_data$first\_name[1])
customer\_data$last\_name <- toupper(customer\_data$last\_name)
customer\_data$last\_name[customer\_data$last\_name == ""] <- ""
customer\_data$last\_name[1] <- sub("^.", tolower(substr(customer\_data$last\_name[1], 1, 1)), customer\_data$last\_name[1])

# Replace missing values in email column
customer\_data$email[is.na(customer\_data$email)] <- "[missing]"

# Replace missing values in phone number column
customer\_data$phone[is.na(customer\_data$phone)] <- "[missing]"

Question 2: Data Analysis (40 points)

Question: Given a dataset of sales information, perform exploratory data analysis to identify any trends or patterns in the data.

Instructions:

* Assume the dataset is named "sales\_data" and is stored in a data frame.
* Assume the dataset contains columns for date, product, region, and sales.
* Assume the date column is in date format and the sales column is in numeric format.

Tasks:

* Calculate the total sales for each product (10 points)
* Calculate the average sales for each region (10 points)
* Create a line plot showing the total sales for each product over time (10 points)
* Create a bar plot showing the average sales for each region (10 points)

Answer:

# Calculate total sales for each product
total\_sales <- aggregate(sales_data$sales, by = list(sales\_data$product), FUN = sum)

# Calculate average sales for each region
avg\_sales\_region <- aggregate(sales\_data$sales, by = list(sales\_data$region), FUN = mean)

# Line plot of total sales for each product over time
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(sales\_data, aes(x = date, y = sales, color = product)) +
geom\_line() +
labs(x = "Date", y = "Total Sales", color = "Product")

# Bar plot of average sales for each region
ggplot(avg\_sales\_region, aes(x = Group.1, y = x, fill = Group.1)) +
geom\_bar(stat = "identity") +
labs(x = "Region", y = "Average Sales")

Question 3: Advanced Analysis (20 points)

Question: Given a dataset of customer information, build a predictive model to identify customers who are at risk of churning.

Instructions:

* Assume the dataset is named "customer\_churn\_data" and is stored in a data frame.
* Assume the dataset contains columns for customer ID, age, gender, income, and churn (a binary variable indicating whether the customer has churned or not).

Tasks:

* Prepare the data for modeling by encoding the gender variable as a factor and splitting the dataset into training and testing sets (10 points)
* Build a logistic regression model to predict churn using the training data (10 points)
* Evaluate the performance of the model on the testing data by calculating the accuracy, precision, and recall (10 points)

Answer:

# Prepare data for modeling
library(caret)
customer\_churn\_data$gender <- as.factor(customer\_churn\_data$gender)
set.seed(123)
train\_index <- createDataPartition(customer\_churn\_data$churn, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train\_data <- customer\_churn\_data[train\_index, ]
test\_data <- customer\_churn\_data[-train\_index, ]

# Build logistic regression model
logistic\_model <- glm(churn ~ ., data = train\_data, family = binomial)

# Evaluate performance of model
predictions <- predict(logistic\_model, test\_data, type = "response")
pred\_class <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)
confusion\_matrix <- table(Predicted = pred\_class, Actual = test\_data$churn)
accuracy <- sum(diag(confusion\_matrix)) / sum(confusion\_matrix)
precision <- sum(diag(confusion\_matrix)) / sum(confusion\_matrix[2,])
recall <- sum(diag(confusion\_matrix)) / sum(confusion\_matrix[,2])

print(accuracy)
print(precision)
print(recall)

instruction: Make a list of desirable Skills for software engineers to add to LinkedIn.
output:
Sure, here is a list of desirable skills for software engineers to add to their LinkedIn profiles:

1. Programming languages: Software engineers should list the programming languages they are proficient in, such as Java, Python, C++, or Ruby. It's also helpful to specify the level of expertise (e.g., beginner, intermediate, or advanced).
2. Web development: Knowledge of front-end and back-end web development technologies, such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Angular, Node.js, or Ruby on Rails, can be valuable skills for software engineers.
3. Databases: Familiarity with database management systems like MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Oracle, or Microsoft SQL Server is important for software engineers, as they often need to design, implement, and manage databases.
4. Cloud computing: As more companies move their operations to the cloud, software engineers who are proficient in cloud computing platforms like Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud Platform (GCP) are in high demand.
5. DevOps: DevOps skills such as continuous integration, continuous delivery, and infrastructure as code can help software engineers streamline the development and deployment process and improve collaboration between development and operations teams.
6. Data structures and algorithms: A strong understanding of data structures (e.g., arrays, linked lists, trees, and graphs) and algorithms (e.g., sorting, searching, and graph traversal) is essential for software engineers to write efficient and scalable code.
7. Software testing and debugging: Software engineers should be familiar with software testing methodologies (e.g., unit testing, integration testing, and acceptance testing) and debugging techniques to ensure the quality of their code.
8. Agile development: Agile development methodologies like Scrum or Kanban are widely used in software development, and software engineers who are familiar with these approaches can work more effectively in cross-functional teams.
9. Version control: Software engineers should be proficient in using version control systems like Git to manage code changes and collaborate with other developers.
10. Problem-solving and critical thinking: Software engineers should have strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills to analyze complex problems, design elegant solutions, and communicate their ideas effectively.

By showcasing these skills on their LinkedIn profiles, software engineers can increase their visibility to potential employers and demonstrate their expertise in the field.

instruction:
You will be asked to answer the following question as part of the LinkedIn Skill assessments.

You find that your project has a tag and branch both named push-notifications, which causes confusion when trying to print out given reference. How can you specify which branch you want to look at?
output:
When dealing with a situation where both a tag and branch in a Git repository have the same name (in this case, "push-notifications"), you can specify which one you want to look at by using Git's built-in reference syntax. To ensure you're looking at the branch and not the tag, you can use the caret (^) character to explicitly reference the branch. Here's how:

To checkout or switch to the "push-notifications" branch, use the following command:

```bash
git checkout push-notifications^
```

The caret (^) character is used to indicate that you want to reference the parent of the commit tagged with "push-notifications". Since tags in Git are not typically moved, the parent commit is most likely the last commit on the "push-notifications" branch.

To confirm that you're on the correct branch, you can use the following command:

```bash
git branch
```

This will display the branches and their relationships, and an asterisk (*) next to the currently active branch. If you see "push-notifications" with an asterisk, you've successfully switched to the desired branch.

If you want to print out the log or history of the "push-notifications" branch, you can use:

```bash
git log push-notifications^..push-notifications
```

This command shows the commits that are only reachable from the "push-notifications" branch, starting from the parent of the tagged commit.

By using the caret (^) character, you can explicitly reference the "push-notifications" branch and avoid confusion with the tag.

instruction:
Write a short description about the given movie or series.

The Witcher (2019)
output:
"The Witcher" is a fantasy drama series based on the book series of the same name by Polish author Andrzej Sapkowski. The series follows the story of Geralt of Rivia, a solitary monster hunter, who struggles to find his place in a world where people often prove more wicked than beasts.

Geralt, played by Henry Cavill, is a witcher, a genetically enhanced human with special powers who hunts monsters for money. He is initially portrayed as a loner, traveling from place to place and killing monsters to earn a living. However, as the series progresses, Geralt becomes entangled in the politics of the Continent, the land where the series is set, and forms bonds with other characters.

One of those characters is Yennefer of Vengerberg, played by Anya Chalotra, a sorceress with a difficult past who is also searching for her place in the world. The series explores Yennefer's origins, her struggles with her powers, and her complicated relationship with Geralt.

The series also introduces Ciri, a young princess with a powerful destiny who becomes entwined in Geralt's fate. Freya Allan plays Ciri, who is on the run from her enemies and seeks Geralt's protection.

"The Witcher" is known for its complex characters, intricate plotlines, and mature themes. The series features graphic violence, sexual content, and strong language, making it more suited for mature audiences. It also boasts impressive special effects, elaborate costumes, and detailed set designs that help bring the world of the Witcher to life.

Overall, "The Witcher" is a dark and gritty fantasy series that explores themes of destiny, identity, and the complexities of human nature. With its compelling characters, intricate plotlines, and stunning visuals, it has quickly become a fan favorite and a critical success.

instruction:
Give examples of popular shows and movies in the genre.

Genre: Crime TV Shows
output:
Certainly! Crime TV shows are among the most popular and enduring genres on television, and there is no shortage of excellent examples to choose from. Here are a few popular crime TV shows from various sub-genres, along with a brief description of each:

1. Law & Order (1990-2010; 2022-present) - This long-running police procedural and legal drama was created by Dick Wolf and follows the detectives of the New York City Police Department as they investigate and solve crimes, and then the prosecutors of the Manhattan District Attorney's Office as they build cases and take the accused to trial. The show is known for its ripped-from-the-headlines plots and its iconic "dun-dun" sound effect.
2. The Sopranos (1999-2007) - Created by David Chase, this groundbreaking crime drama follows the life of New Jersey mob boss Tony Soprano as he struggles to balance his family life with his criminal empire. The show is known for its complex characters, deep themes, and innovative storytelling.
3. The Wire (2002-2008) - Created by David Simon, this critically acclaimed crime drama explores the drug trade in Baltimore from multiple perspectives, including the police, the politicians, and the drug dealers themselves. The show is known for its gritty realism, intricate plotting, and deep social commentary.
4. Breaking Bad (2008-2013) - Created by Vince Gilligan, this Emmy-winning crime drama follows high school chemistry teacher Walter White as he turns to cooking and selling methamphetamine after being diagnosed with terminal lung cancer. The show is known for its intense character development, suspenseful plotting, and stunning visual style.
5. Narcos (2015-2017) - This Netflix original crime drama tells the true story of the rise and fall of Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar and the Medellín Cartel. The show is known for its intense action, vivid historical detail, and strong performances.
6. Mindhunter (2017-2019) - Created by Joe Penhall, this crime drama follows the early days of the FBI's Behavioral Science Unit, as agents Holden Ford and Bill Tench interview notorious serial killers to understand their motives and develop new criminal profiling techniques. The show is known for its atmospheric style, thoughtful character development, and exploration of complex psychological themes.
7. Lupin (2021-present) - This French-language Netflix original series follows the adventures of master thief Assane Diop, who uses his skills to take down the wealthy and powerful people who have wronged him and his family. The show is known for its stylish visuals, clever plotting, and charismatic lead performance.

These are just a few examples of the many excellent crime TV shows out there. Whether you prefer police procedurals, legal dramas, mob stories, or heist thrillers, there is something for everyone in this popular and enduring genre.

instruction:
In what genres does the given movie or series fall?

Manifest
output:
"Manifest" is a television series that falls into several genres, including:

1. Drama: The series follows the lives of the passengers and crew of a commercial airline flight that suddenly reappears after being missing for five years. The show explores the emotional and psychological impact of this experience on the characters, as well as their relationships with each other and the world they left behind.

2. Science Fiction: The central mystery of the series revolves around the unexplained phenomenon that caused the plane to disappear and reappear. This element of the show is rooted in science fiction, as the characters and the audience are left to speculate about the possible explanations for what happened.

3. Mystery: The series is also a mystery, as the characters try to unravel the truth about what happened to them and why. There are many questions that need to be answered, and the show keeps viewers guessing with each new episode.

4. Supernatural: As the series progresses, it becomes clear that there are supernatural elements at play. The passengers of the plane begin to experience strange visions and hear voices that seem to be guiding them towards certain actions. This adds a layer of intrigue and suspense to the show.

5. Thriller: With its tense and suspenseful plot, "Manifest" can also be a classified as a thriller. There are many twists and turns throughout the series, and the stakes are high for the characters as they try to uncover the truth about their situation.

Overall, "Manifest" is a complex and engaging series that blends elements of drama, science fiction, mystery, and supernatural thriller to create a unique and captivating viewing experience.

instruction:
Give a brief description of the given category of movies and shows.

Period Dramas
output:
Period dramas are a category of films and television shows that are set in a particular historical period, typically before the mid-20th century. These productions aim to realistically depict the past, focusing on aspects such as fashion, language, customs, and social norms of the time.

The settings for period dramas can vary greatly, ranging from the courts of European monarchies to the frontier towns of the American West, from the bustling streets of Victorian London to the tranquil countryside estates of Edwardian England. The narratives often explore significant historical events, figures, or movements, providing context and insight into the past.

Period dramas can be further divided into several sub-genres, including:

1. Historical dramas: These films and shows focus on specific events, figures, or periods in history, such as the French Revolution, the American Civil War, or the reign of Queen Elizabeth I. They often involve extensive research to ensure historical accuracy and can provide educational value alongside entertainment.
2. Costume dramas: This sub-genre places a strong emphasis on the visual aspects of a period, particularly the fashion and design elements. Costume dramas often showcase elaborate and meticulously researched costumes, sets, and locations to transport the audience to a different time and place.
3. Romantic period dramas: These productions focus on the relationships and romantic entanglements of characters set against a historical backdrop. They can range from sweeping, epic love stories to more intimate portrayals of courtship and marriage.
4. Family sagas: These period dramas follow the lives and experiences of multiple generations of a family over an extended period. They often explore themes of inheritance, legacy, and the passage of time, providing a rich and detailed portrait of a family's evolution.
5. Mystery and detective period dramas: Set in a historical context, these productions feature mysteries to be solved or crimes to be investigated by protagonists such as Sherlock Holmes or Miss Marple. The combination of historical detail and intrigue can make for captivating storytelling.
6. Adaptations: Many period dramas are based on classic novels, plays, or other literary works set in a specific historical period. These adaptations can introduce new audiences to timeless stories while providing a visually engaging representation of the source material.

Period dramas have maintained their popularity due to their ability to transport audiences to different times and places, offering a glimpse into the lives and experiences of people from the past. The genre provides a rich and diverse tapestry of stories, settings, and characters, allowing for endless possibilities in storytelling.

instruction: Provide an example of how a table of contents can be generated automatically in a LATEX document. Make sure your example contains the necessary commands.
Imagine you have a FEN notation of a chessboard. How can you draw a board to show this state of the chessboard in LATEX? Create a LATEX code that includes all the required commands and packages.
output:
To draw a chessboard in LaTeX representing a given FEN (Forsyth-Edwards Notation) notation, you can use the `skak` package along with the `xskak` package. Here's a detailed explanation and an example:

1. Install TeX distribution (if not already installed). I recommend using TeXLive: <https://www.tug.org/texlive/>
2. Create a new LaTeX document (`.tex` file). You can use any text editor.
3. Add the following lines to the preamble of your LaTeX document to include the required packages:

```latex
\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{skak, xskak, chessboard}
\usepackage{geometry} % This package is optional. It's used to adjust the page layout.
\geometry{margin=1in} % This line is optional. It sets the margin to 1 inch.
```

4. Define a command to set up the chessboard according to the FEN notation. Replace `your_fen_notation` with the desired FEN notation:

```latex
\newcommand{\setchessboard}[1]{%
  \newgame
  \setboardfontsize{12pt}% Adjust the font size as needed
  \setboard{start}
  \readboardfile{#1.cbf}%
}
```

5. Create a board image file (`.cbf`) for the given FEN notation using the `texmate` tool. For example, if your FEN notation is `rnbqkbnr/pppppppp/8/8/8/8/PPPPPPPP/RNBQKBNR`, you should create a file named `rnbqkbnrpppppppp888888PPPPPPPPRNBQKBNR.cbf` with the following content:

```
[board]
a8=wR, b8=wN, c8=wB, d8=wQ, e8=wK, f8=wB, g8=wN, h8=wR,
a7=bR, b7=bP, c7=bP, d7=bP, e7=bP, f7=bP, g7=bP, h7=bP,
a6=0, b6=0, c6=0, d6=0, e6=0, f6=0, g6=0, h6=0,
a5=0, b5=0, c5=0, d5=0, e5=0, f5=0, g5=0, h5=0,
a4=0, b4=0, c4=0, d4=0, e4=0, f4=0, g4=0, h4=0,
a3=0, b3=0, c3=0, d3=0, e3=0, f3=0, g3=0, h3=0,
a2=bP, b2=bP, c2=bP, d2=bP, e2=bP, f2=bP, g2=bP, h2=bP,
a1=wR, b1=wN, c1=wB, d1=wQ, e1=wK, f1=wB, g1=wN, h1=wR,
```

6. Now, you can use the defined command in the main content of your LaTeX document:

```latex
\begin{document}

\setchessboard{rnbqkbnrpppppppp888888PPPPPPPPRNBQKBNR}

\chessboard

The given LaTeX code will generate a simple, basic format of a document with no additional packages or parameters. The output of this code will be a single page document consisting of the text "First document. This is a simple example, with no extra parameters or packages included." centered in the middle of the page.

The document class used is "article" which is the most basic and commonly used document class in LaTeX. It provides a standard format for typesetting articles, reports, and other similar documents.

The text inside the "document" environment is the body of the document. In this case, the text is a simple sentence that describes the purpose of the document and serves as an example for beginners who are new to LaTeX.

Overall, this LaTeX code produces a clean and simple document that is easy to read and understand.

instruction:
Write a simple guide for uploading the bibliography database
 on Overleaf.
output:
Sure, I'd be happy to help you upload your bibliography database to Overleaf! Here's a simple guide to follow:

1. **Prepare your bibliography database:** Before you can upload your bibliography database to Overleaf, you need to make sure it's in the correct format. Overleaf supports various bibliography styles, including BibTeX, BibLaTeX, and Mendeley. The most common format is BibTeX, which uses the `.bib` file extension. Make sure your bibliography database is saved as a `.bib` file.
2. **Create a new project in Overleaf:** If you haven't already, create a new project in Overleaf by clicking on the "New Project" button on the Overleaf homepage.
3. **Upload your bibliography database:** Once you've created a new project, you can upload your bibliography database by clicking on the "Upload" button in the top left corner of the project window. Select your `.bib` file and click "Open" to upload it.
4. **Add the bibliography database to your LaTeX document:** To use your bibliography database in your LaTeX document, you need to add a few lines of code to the preamble of your document. Here's an example using BibTeX:

```latex
\usepackage{cite} % include this line if you're using BibTeX
\bibliographystyle{plain} % choose a bibliography style
\addbibresource{mybibliography.bib} % replace "mybibliography" with the name of your .bib file
```

The `\bibliographystyle` command sets the style of the bibliography, and the `\addbibresource` command tells LaTeX where to find the bibliography database.

5. **Cite sources in your LaTeX document:** To cite sources from your bibliography database, use the `\cite` command followed by the citation key. For example:

```latex
This is a sentence that cites a source \cite{smith2020example}.
```

Make sure the citation key matches the key used in your bibliography database.

6. **Generate the bibliography:** Once you've added the necessary code to your LaTeX document, you can generate the bibliography by clicking on the "Recompile" button in Overleaf. The bibliography will appear at the end of your document.

That's it! I hope this guide helps you upload and use your bibliography database in Overleaf. Let me know if you have any questions or need further assistance.

instruction:
How would you describe the meaning of this lyrics? Describe some of the metaphors.

My lover’s got humor
She’s the giggle at a funeral
Knows everybody’s disapproval
I should’ve worshipped her sooner
output:
These lyrics describe a lover who has a unique and unexpected sense of humor, using several metaphors to convey this idea.

The first metaphor, "She's the giggle at a funeral," suggests that she is able to find humor or lightness in even the most solemn or serious situations. This could imply that she has a way of lifting the mood or bringing a fresh perspective to difficult or challenging circumstances. It's important to note that this metaphor is not intended to make light of funerals or the grieving process, but rather to highlight the lover's ability to bring a sense of joy and laughter to those around her.

The second metaphor, "Knows everybody's disapproval," suggests that the lover is aware of the judgments or disapproval of others, but is not deterred by it. This could imply that she is confident in herself and her choices, and is not overly concerned with what others think of her. It's possible that this trait is part of what makes her so funny and unique, as she is not afraid to challenge social norms or expectations.

The final line, "I should've worshipped her sooner," is a declaration of the speaker's admiration and appreciation for the lover. It suggests that the speaker has come to realize the value and worth of the lover's unique qualities, and wishes that they had recognized and appreciated her sooner. Overall, these lyrics celebrate a lover who is confident, funny, and able to find joy and lightness in even the most challenging situations.

instruction: Suggest some Audiobooks for First-Time Listeners. You can attract them by choosing popular works.
output:

1. "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone" by J.K. Rowling, narrated by Jim Dale - This is a classic fantasy novel that has been enjoyed by people of all ages. Jim Dale's narration is excellent, and he does a fantastic job of bringing the characters to life.
2. "The Help" by Kathryn Stockett, narrated by Octavia Spencer, Jenna Lamia, and Bahni Turpin - This is a powerful and moving novel about race, class, and friendship in the American South during the 1960s. The audiobook is narrated by three talented actresses, including Octavia Spencer, who won an Academy Award for her portrayal of Minny in the film adaptation.

Wilfred Watters's narration is energetic and engaging, and he does an excellent job of capturing the excitement and adventure of the story.
4. "The Night Circus" by Erin Morgenstern, narrated by Jim Dale - This is a magical and enchanting novel about a mysterious circus that appears without warning and disappears just as suddenly. I hope these suggestions are helpful, and that you find a audiobook that you enjoy!

instruction: Make a list of the most popular podcasts.
output:
Sure, I'd be happy to help! Here are some of the most popular podcasts, based on various metrics such as listener ratings, download numbers, and cultural impact:

1. "Serial" - This investigative journalism podcast was created by the team behind "This American Life" and quickly became a cultural phenomenon when it debuted in 2014. The first season follows the case of Adnan Syed, a man convicted of murdering his ex-girlfriend in 1999. The show's deep dive into the case raised questions about the reliability of Syed's conviction and helped spur a wave of true crime podcasts.
2. "The Daily" - Produced by The New York Times, "The Daily" is a news podcast that covers major stories happening around the world. Each episode is around 20 minutes long and features interviews with Times journalists and other experts. "The Daily" has a large following and is often credited with helping to popularize the daily news podcast format.
3. "Stuff You Should Know" - Hosted by Chuck Bryant and Josh Clark, "Stuff You Should Know" is a general knowledge podcast that covers a wide range of topics, from science and history to pop culture and current events. With over 1,500 episodes and a dedicated fan base, "Stuff You Should Know" is one of the most popular podcasts of all time.
4. "Radiolab" - This podcast, produced by WNYC Studios, is known for its innovative storytelling and deep dives into scientific and philosophical topics. Hosted by Jad Abumrad and Robert Krulwich, "Radiolab" has won numerous awards for its unique approach to audio storytelling.
5. "My Favorite Murder" - Hosted by Karen Kilgariff and Georgia Hardstark, "My Favorite Murder" is a true crime comedy podcast that has gained a large following since its debut in 2016. The show features the hosts discussing their favorite murder cases, as well as interviews with experts and other true crime enthusiasts.
6. "How I Built This" - Hosted by Guy Raz, "How I Built This" is a business podcast that explores the stories behind some of the world's most successful companies. Each episode features an interview with a founder or CEO, who shares the challenges and triumphs they faced while building their business.
7. "The Joe Rogan Experience" - Hosted by comedian and TV host Joe Rogan, "The Joe Rogan Experience" is a long-form conversation podcast that covers a wide range of topics, from politics and science to comedy and spirituality. With over 1,700 episodes and a massive following, "The Joe Rogan Experience" is one of the most popular podcasts in the world.

These are just a few examples of the many popular podcasts out there. There are podcasts for just about every interest and niche, so there's something out there for everyone!

instruction:
You should take the time to read the situation and problem carefully and give the best advice you can.

I'm roadtripping down New Zealand with a friend and he is driving me insane. He hasn't enjoyed all the tours we've been on and doesn't want to chat about ANYTHING. We're a week into our trip and this is the quietest I've ever been when hanging out with another person as he rebuffs all my attempts at conversation. He wears his airpods in the car and around the accommodation - not that I've kept trying to talk to him. Honestly, he is bringing some seriously bad vibes to everything and it's ruining my trip.
We don't have anything booked in yet for the South Island, so I'm planning to ditch him in Wellington at the ferry. Probably book my own car in Picton and just relax alone for the rest of the trip. (Just thinking about it is such sharp relief that it makes me feel emotional. I'll pay whatever price I have to just to get away from him.)
Obviously telling him he is ruining my trip with his negative energy isn't the best way to approach this.
Any advice for the best approach so we can split amicably? Or at least not have an argument that ends with very hurt feelings?
output:
I understand that this is a challenging situation for you, and it's important to handle it with care to maintain your friendship. Here's a step-by-step approach to help you navigate this situation:

1. Self-reflection: Before addressing the issue with your friend, take some time to reflect on your own behavior. Ensure that you have not unintentionally done anything to upset or offend him. This reflection will help you approach the conversation with an open mind and a constructive attitude.

2. Choose the right time and place: Find a quiet, private, and comfortable setting to have this conversation. Make sure you both have enough time to talk without feeling rushed or interrupted.

3. Start with a positive: Begin the conversation by acknowledging the good parts of your trip and your friendship. This will help create a positive atmosphere and make it easier for your friend to receive constructive feedback.

4. Express your feelings tactfully: Use "I" statements to express your feelings without blaming or criticizing your friend. For example, "I feel lonely and disappointed when I can't share conversations and experiences with you during our trip."

5. Share your observations: Describe the specific behaviors that have been causing your feelings, such as his lack of participation in tours and constant use of airpods. Be factual and avoid making assumptions or judgments.

6. Listen actively: Give your friend the opportunity to share his perspective and feelings. Be empathetic and try to understand his point of view.

7. Seek a solution together: Work together to find a solution that respects both of your needs and desires. For example, you could suggest spending some time apart during the day to pursue individual interests and then reconvening in the evening to share experiences. Alternatively, you could discuss finding activities that you both enjoy and can participate in together.

8. Set boundaries: If your friend continues to behave in a way that negatively impacts your experience, it's essential to set boundaries. Politely but firmly communicate your expectations and the consequences of not meeting them.

9. Be prepared for different outcomes: Your friend may respond positively and make an effort to improve the situation, or he may not. Regardless of his reaction, it's crucial to stay true to yourself and prioritize your emotional well-being.

10. Follow through: If, after attempting to address the issue, you still feel the need to separate from your friend, follow through with your plan. Be honest, respectful, and kind when communicating your decision, but remain firm.

By following this approach, you increase the chances of having a productive conversation and finding a solution that works for both of you. Remember to prioritize your emotional well-being and maintain open communication throughout the process.

instruction:
You are given a description that provides a set of facts or a scenario. It is up to you to craft a story from these facts and scenarios. The missing pieces must be filled in with imaginative but logical information.

Ten European football teams – the Netherlands, England, Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and Wales – will participate in a season-long “OneLove” campaign promoting inclusion and opposing discrimination.
output:
Once upon a time, in the bustling world of European football, a group of ten passionate teams came together with a shared vision of promoting inclusion and combating discrimination. These teams included the Netherlands, England, Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland, and Wales. They decided to embark on a season-long "OneLove" campaign to spread their message and inspire positive change both on and off the field.

The "OneLove" campaign was born out of a desire to create a more welcoming and inclusive environment for all football fans, regardless of their race, religion, gender, or sexual orientation. The participating teams recognized that football was more than just a game – it was a powerful platform that could be used to break down barriers and unite people from all walks of life.

Throughout the season, the "OneLove" teams wore special armbands during their matches, featuring a heart-shaped design and the words "OneLove" emblazoned across the front. These armbands served as a visual reminder of the teams' commitment to promoting diversity, equality, and inclusion.

In addition to wearing the armbands, the teams also took part in various events and initiatives aimed at raising awareness about the importance of inclusion and diversity. These ranged from hosting workshops and panel discussions to creating educational resources for schools and community groups.

The "OneLove" campaign was met with widespread acclaim and support from football fans and organizations around the world. Many other teams and clubs expressed interest in joining the campaign and adopting its values and principles.

As the season drew to a close, the "OneLove" teams reflected on the impact of their campaign. While there was still much work to be done, they were proud of the progress they had made in promoting inclusion and combating discrimination in the world of football. And they knew that, as long as they continued to work together and stand up for what was right, they could make a real difference in the lives of countless people.

And so, the "OneLove" campaign lived on, inspiring a new generation of football players and fans to embrace diversity, promote inclusion, and stand up against discrimination in all its forms.

instruction:
Please suggest a few papers to consider based on the search term given. The names of the papers should be listed.

Topic: scaling law + machine learning
output:
Sure, here are some papers related to the topic of "scaling law + machine learning":

1. "Deep Learning Scaling is Predictable, Empirically" by Henry W. Lin, Pang Wei Koh, and Kevin Swersky. This paper explores the scaling properties of deep neural networks and proposes a power-law relationship between model size, dataset size, and generalization performance.
2. "On Large Minibatch Training for Deep Learning: Generalization Gap and Sharp Minima" by Nitish Shirish Keskar, Dheevatsa Mudigere, Jorge Nocedal, Mikhail Smelyanskiy, and Ping Tak Peter Tang. This paper investigates the impact of minibatch size on the training and generalization of deep neural networks and proposes a scaling law for controlling the sharpness of minima.
3. "Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models" by Anna Goldberg, Edouard Grave, Mark A. Ritter, and Alexandra Mikhaleva. This paper studies the scaling properties of neural language models and proposes a scaling law for predicting the performance of models trained on different dataset sizes.
4. "The Scaling Laws of Neural Language Model Training" by Nitish Shirish Keskar, Dheevatsa Mudigere, Jorge Nocedal, Mikhail Smelyanskiy, and Ping Tak Peter Tang. This paper extends the analysis of the previous paper and investigates the scaling properties of more complex neural language models, such as transformer models.
5. "Scaling Laws for Multi-Task Deep Learning" by Anna Goldberg, Edouard Grave, Mark A. Ritter, and Alexandra Mikhaleva. This paper studies the scaling properties of multi-task deep learning models and proposes a scaling law for predicting the performance of models trained on multiple tasks simultaneously.
6. "Scaling Laws for Vision and Language Models" by Yinhan Liu, Myle Ott, Naman Goyal, Jingfei Du, Mandar Joshi, Danqi Chen, Omer Levy, Mike Lewis, Luke Zettlemoyer, and Douwe Kiela. This paper studies the scaling properties of vision and language models and proposes a scaling law for predicting the performance of models trained on vision and language tasks.
7. "A Theory of Deep Learning via Hierarchical Convolutional Networks" by Yann LeCun, Yoshua Bengio, and Geoffrey Hinton. This paper proposes a hierarchical convolutional network model and derives a scaling law for the number of parameters required to achieve a given level of performance.
8. "Scaling Laws for Neural Network Approximation and Learning" by Nati Srebro, Shai Shalev-Shwartz, and Nathan Srebro. This paper derives scaling laws for the number of samples required to learn a neural network function to a given accuracy, based on the number of parameters and the complexity of the target function.

instruction:
A job description is a document that clearly states the requirements, duties, responsibilities, and skills required to perform a specific role. You need to write a job description based on the given information. You should describe responsibilities and duties in your job description in a logical order. Remember that potential employees often get their first impression of your company from your job description. So, the job description should convey the attractiveness of your company.

Company: [Company name]
Job: Software Engineer
Job Responsibilities:
Receive and perform code reviews with other engineers.
Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests to verify functionality using automated testing frameworks such as Pytest.
Work collaboratively with fellow software engineers to build features requested by business stakeholders.
Participate in Agile teams to develop, test, and debug complex data processing pipelines and data analysis applications using big data processing systems such as Apache Spark.
Diagnose, debug, and perform root cause analysis of issues and defects.
Skills and experience that will lead to success:
Experience with a modern software programming language. We use Python, and Pyspark extensively.
Experience with cloud systems like AWS or Azure
Experience writing SQL queries 
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Eagerness to work in a highly-collaborative environment
Some visualization experience such as Tableau or PowerBI is helpful
output:
**Job Title: Software Engineer**

**Company:** [Company Name]

**Job Description:**

We are seeking a talented and motivated Software Engineer to join our dynamic team. The Software Engineer will be responsible for writing and testing code, collaborating with other engineers to build features, debugging and resolving issues, and contributing to our data processing pipelines and data analysis applications.

**Responsibilities:**

1. Receive and perform code reviews with other engineers to ensure high-quality code and adherence to best practices.
2. Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests to verify functionality using automated testing frameworks such as Pytest.
3. Collaborate with fellow software engineers to build features requested by business stakeholders.
4. Participate in Agile teams to develop, test, and debug complex data processing pipelines and data analysis applications using big data processing systems such as Apache Spark.
5. Diagnose, debug, and perform root cause analysis of issues and defects.
6. Write SQL queries to extract and manipulate data.
7. Create visualizations using tools such as Tableau or PowerBI.

**Requirements:**

1. Experience with a modern software programming language, with a strong preference for Python and PySpark.
2. Experience with cloud systems like AWS or Azure.
3. Strong written and verbal communication skills.
4. Eagerness to work in a highly-collaborative environment.
5. Experience writing SQL queries.
6. Experience with big data processing systems such as Apache Spark is a plus.
7. Visualization experience using tools such as Tableau or PowerBI is helpful.

**Why Join Us?**

At [Company Name], we value our employees and offer a dynamic and inclusive work environment. We provide opportunities for growth and development, a competitive salary and benefits package, and a commitment to work-life balance. If you are passionate about software engineering and excited about the opportunity to work with a talented team of professionals, we encourage you to apply for this position.

We look forward to hearing from you!

instruction:
Write a section for a blog post and try to cover all of the provided information about this section in your text.

Blog Topic: 7 Fall Fashion Trends Worth Taking on Your Trip
Section Title: Trusty Jeans
Main point of the section: jeans can be worn all year and look good with everything .
output:
Section: Trusty Jeans

When it comes to fall fashion, there is one staple that never goes out of style: trusty jeans. Jeans are a versatile and timeless piece of clothing that can be worn all year round, but they are especially perfect for the fall season. Not only do they provide the right amount of warmth for those cooler autumn days, but they also pair well with almost any top or shoe in your wardrobe.

One of the reasons jeans are such a popular choice for fall fashion is their ability to be dressed up or down. For a more casual look, pair your jeans with a cozy sweater and some sneakers. If you're looking to dress up your jeans, try pairing them with a blouse and a pair of ankle boots. The options are endless, making jeans a must-have in any travel wardrobe.

Another reason jeans are a great choice for travel is their durability. Jeans are made to last, so you won't have to worry about them wearing out or getting damaged easily. This makes them a practical and reliable choice for a long trip. Plus, jeans are easy to care for and can be washed and dried quickly, so you won't have to spend a lot of time doing laundry on your trip.

When it comes to selecting the perfect pair of jeans for your fall trip, there are a few things to consider. First, think about the wash and color. Darker washes and colors, such as indigo or black, tend to be more versatile and can be dressed up or down. Lighter washes and colors, such as white or faded blue, are better suited for more casual looks.

Next, consider the style of the jeans. Skinny jeans are always a popular choice, but straight leg and wide leg styles are also on trend this fall. Bootcut jeans are also a great option for fall, as they can be easily paired with boots.

In terms of fabric, denim is the obvious choice for jeans, but there are also options such as jeggings, which are made with a stretchy fabric that provides more comfort and flexibility.

In conclusion, jeans are a versatile and durable choice for fall fashion, making them the perfect addition to any travel wardrobe. Whether you prefer skinny jeans, straight leg, or bootcut, there is a pair of jeans out there that will suit your style and needs. So don't be afraid to pack a few pairs of trusty jeans on your next fall trip.

instruction:
Using the provided topic as a starting point, brainstorm ideas for videos that can be made about it on YouTube.

In spite of the fact that procrastination feels bad to you, why do you do it?
output:
1. "The Psychology of Procrastination: Why We Do It and How to Stop" - This video could explore the underlying reasons why people procrastinate, such as fear of failure, perfectionism, or lack of motivation. It could also provide strategies for overcoming procrastination and increasing productivity.
2. "The Science of Procrastination: A Neurochemical Perspective" - This video could delve into the neurochemical processes that contribute to procrastination, such as the role of dopamine and the brain's reward system. It could also discuss how to hack these processes to reduce procrastination and increase focus.
3. "Procrastination and Mental Health: Breaking the Cycle" - This video could examine the link between procrastination and mental health issues such as anxiety and depression. It could offer tips for managing these conditions and reducing procrastination as a result.
4. "Productivity Hacks: How to Stop Procrastinating and Get Things Done" - This video could provide practical tips and tools for overcoming procrastination, such as time-blocking, the Pomodoro technique, and accountability partners.
5. "The Art of Prioritization: How to Decide What's Worth Doing (and What's Not)" - This video could help viewers develop a system for prioritizing tasks and projects, so they can focus on what's truly important and avoid falling into the trap of procrastination.
6. "The Role of Environment in Procrastination: How Your Surroundings Affect Your Productivity" - This video could explore how factors such as clutter, noise, and lighting can impact procrastination and offer tips for creating a more productive workspace.
7. "Procrastination and Habit Formation: How to Build Good Habits and Break Bad Ones" - This video could discuss the science of habit formation and offer strategies for building good habits that can help reduce procrastination.
8. "The Power of Small Wins: How to Build Momentum and Overcome Procrastination" - This video could emphasize the importance of breaking tasks down into smaller, more manageable pieces and celebrating small victories along the way to build momentum and overcome procrastination.
9. "The Impact of Procrastination on Relationships: How Putting Things Off Can Hurt Your Personal and Professional Life" - This video could examine the ways in which procrastination can negatively impact relationships with friends, family, and coworkers, and offer strategies for communicating more effectively and managing expectations.
10. "The Procrastination Paradox: How Putting Things Off Can Sometimes Lead to Better Results" - This video could explore the idea that procrastination isn't always bad, and that sometimes allowing ideas to marinate or taking breaks can lead to more creative and innovative solutions. However, it could also emphasize the importance of setting boundaries and avoiding excessive procrastination that can lead to missed deadlines and other negative consequences.

instruction:
Find synonyms for the given word. You need to write down how the provided synonyms differ from the original word in terms of meaning, usage, etc.

adversity
output:
Sure, I'd be happy to help you find synonyms for the word "adversity" and explain how they differ in meaning and usage.

1. Hardship: This word refers to difficult situations or conditions, often related to a lack of resources or difficult circumstances. While similar to adversity, hardship often implies a prolonged period of suffering, whereas adversity can be a single event or ongoing.
2. Misfortune: This word refers to bad luck or unfortunate events that happen to someone. Misfortune is often used to describe events that are outside of a person's control, whereas adversity can refer to challenges that are self-inflicted or beyond a person's control.
3. Tribulation: This word refers to a state of great distress or suffering, often caused by difficult circumstances or events. Tribulation is a more intense form of adversity, often used to describe severe or catastrophic events.
4. Calamity: This word refers to a disastrous event causing great and often sudden damage or distress. Calamity is similar to tribulation, but it often implies a more sudden and devastating event, such as a natural disaster or accident.
5. Affliction: This word refers to a state of pain, suffering, or distress, often caused by a medical condition or mental health issue. Affliction is similar to adversity, but it often implies a more personal or internal struggle.
6. Ordeal: This word refers to a prolonged period of suffering or hardship, often caused by a difficult or challenging situation. Ordeal is similar to adversity, but it often implies a sense of testing or endurance, as if the person is being put through a difficult experience to prove their strength or resilience.
7. Setback: This word refers to a delay or obstacle that makes progress or achievement more difficult. Setbacks are often temporary and can be overcome with effort, whereas adversity can be more long-term and require significant resources or changes to overcome.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

instruction:
Discuss the origins and history of the word that has been given to you.

oblique
output:
The word "oblique" has its origins in Latin and Old French. The Latin word "obliquus" means "slanting, sloping, or at an angle," and it is derived from the verb "obligare," which means "to bend against." The Old French word "oblique" was adopted into English in the late 14th or early 15th century.

The word "oblique" has several meanings and uses in English. It can be used as an adjective to describe something that is not in a straight line or direction, but rather slanting or at an angle. For example, an oblique line is one that is not parallel or perpendicular to another line, but rather at an angle to it.

In mathematics, an oblique triangle is one that does not have a right angle, and an oblique cylinder is one that is not a right cylinder.

In grammar, an oblique case is a grammatical case that is used to indicate various roles, such as the indirect object of a verb, in contrast to the subject or direct object cases.

In a more metaphorical sense, "oblique" can also describe something that is not straightforward or direct, but rather indirect or evasive. For example, an oblique comment or remark may be intended to convey a subtle or indirect message.

Historically, the word "oblique" has been used in various contexts to describe a range of phenomena, from mathematical concepts to linguistic forms to social behaviors. Despite its varied uses, the word continues to convey the basic idea of something that is not straight, direct, or obvious, but rather slanting or indirect in some way.
